zh.Rada, Santander, Cantabria, España: 西班牙坎塔布里亞桑坦德
zh.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA於大約1170年出生在西班牙。他和Tereza de AZAGRA至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.Rada, Santander, Cantabria, España: 西班牙坎塔布里亞桑坦德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA于大约1170年出生在西班牙。他和Tereza de AZAGRA至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: RADA
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Delfin, Ernesto, Jose, Lilia, Adelardo, Alipio, Arquimedes, Arturo, Cesar, Cruz, Eladia.

Czech and Slovak: from a short form of any of various Slavic personal names containing the element rad ‘merry, glad’ (from Old Slavic radъ), such as Radoslav, in which the second element means ‘glory’ or ‘famous’.
Hungarian: from the old personal name Rada or Ráda, of Slavic origin (see 1 above and Rad ).

variants.name: Rach
meanings: German: from Middle High German rach ‘rough, stiff’, a nickname for someone affected by hard work, or a topographic name from the same word in the sense ‘steep, rough’.
Czech, Polish, and Sorbian: from a pet form of the personal names beginning with Ra-, especially Slavic names such as Czech Radoslav, Polish and Sorbian Radosław (see Rada ).

variants.name: Radach
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Christoph, Otto, Waltraut.
German: from a short form of any of various Slavic personal names based on Old Slavic radъ ‘merry, glad’ (see Rada 1).
Americanized form of Czech Radáč: from a pet form of a personal name containing the Slavic element rad ‘merry, glad’ (see Rada 1).

variants.name: Rad
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ali, Bijan, Abbas, Amad, Masoud, Nader, Nasser, Saeed, Ahmad, Alireza, Amir, Azin.

Iranian: from Persian rād ‘bold, brave’, also ‘skilful, learned’, forming the second part (originally a suffix) of compound names such as Mahdavirad or Mahdavi Rad (see Mahdavi ). The surname may also be from the personal name Rād, based on the same word. Compare Raad 2.
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with rād, rāt ‘advice, counsel’.

variants.name: Radack
meanings: German: from a Germanized pet form of any of various Slavic personal names based on Old Slavic radъ ‘merry, glad’ (see Rada 1, compare Radak ).
Americanized form of Serbian and Croatian   Radak  .

History: The progenitor of many of the American Radacks was Ephraim Radack, a German from Alt Danzig in the Russian Empire (today Karlivka in Ukraine), a settlement founded by the colonists from Danzig in Prussia (today Gdansk in Poland). He and his family immigrated to SD in the 1870s.

nameMeanings.name: Lope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Late Latin name Lupus ‘wolf’, probably adopted as a calque of the Germanic name Wolf .

nameMeanings.name: Ortiz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Miguel, Angel, Jorge, Pedro, Ramon.
Spanish: patronymic from the Basque personal name Orti, from Latin Fortunius (see Fortuno ). Compare Urtiz .

History: Nicolas Ortiz Ladron de Guevara, a native of Mexico City, came to Santa Fe, NM, in June 1694 with his wife and children and other families recruited to recolonize New Mexico after the Pueblo Indian revolt.

variants.name: Fortuno
meanings: Latin: A female name derived from Latin fortuna, id. Fortuna was the goddess of fortune, and from her hand were derived riches and poverty, pleasures and misfortunes, blessings and pains.

variants.name: Orquiz
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): perhaps an altered form of Spanish   Ortiz  .

variants.name: Ortis
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a patronymic derived from the genitive case of the medieval given name Ote or Ode (of Norman, and ultimately Germanic, origin; compare Odette ). In northern England the medieval given name survived into the 19th century. It came to be used as a given name in America in honour of the Revolutionary hero James Otis ( 1725–83 ); in modern times it has been bestowed in honour of the American soul singer Otis Redding ( 1941–67 ).

variants.name: Ortez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Ana, Carlos, Margarita, Mario, Sergio, Adela, Adriana, Amando, Aminta, Andres.
Spanish: variant of Ortiz or of Catalan Ortes, plural form of Orta .

variants.name: Urtiz
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Spanish   Ortiz  .

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA was born about 1170, in Spain. He had at least 1 son with Tereza de AZAGRA.

fr.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA est né environ 1170, à Espagne. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Tereza de AZAGRA.

de.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA wurde um 1170 in Spanien geboren. Er hatte mit Tereza de AZAGRA mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA è nato il circa 1170 nel Spagna. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Tereza de AZAGRA.

ja.Rada, Santander, Cantabria, España: スペインカンタブリアサンタンダー
ja.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADAは約1170年にスペインで生まれた。彼はTereza de AZAGRAとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA(은)는 대략 1170년 스페인에서 태어났다. 그는 Tereza de AZAGRA(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA nasceu aproximadamente 1170, em Espanha. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Tereza de AZAGRA.

ru.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA родился около 1170 г. в местности Испания. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Tereza de AZAGRA.

es.lifeSummary: Lope Ortiz de RADA nació aproximadamente 1170, en España. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Tereza de AZAGRA.
